steps.title: Identify condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content:
• The system has infinitely many solutions, so the two equations are scalar multiples.
  • Thus there exists a nonzero constant k such that
$$A = k\cdot120,\quad B = k\cdot84,\quad C = k\cdot76$$

steps.title: Compute (A+C)/B and simplify
steps.content:
• Form the expression (A+C)/B and substitute:
$$\frac{A+C}{B} = \frac{k\cdot120 + k\cdot76}{k\cdot84} = \frac{k(120+76)}{k\cdot84} = \frac{196}{84}$$
  • Reduce the fraction:
$$\frac{196}{84} = \frac{49}{21} = \frac{7}{3}$$The value does not depend on k, so (A+C)/B = 7/3.
The answer is 49/21.

Equivalent forms accepted: 49/21, 2.333, 7/3.
